nodes.title: Teaching Assistantship
nodes.description: Undergraduate student service as a teaching assistant, usually in an introductory-level course in the discipline, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.

nodes.title: Music and Mental Health
nodes.description: See Schedule of Courses for specific titles.

nodes.title: Horror in film Psych/Neuro/Soc
nodes.description: See Schedule of Courses for specific titles.

nodes.title: Undergraduate Research
nodes.description: Undergraduate student work on individual or small team research projects under the supervision of a faculty member, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.
nodes.other_requirements: Prior permission from Dr. Copeland required to enroll

nodes.title: Healthy Brains, Healthy Bodies
nodes.description: Introduces students to the science of brain health and supporting emotional wellbeing during the transition to college. Critically examines the evidence supporting wellness behaviors such as mindfulness, exercise, nutrition, relationships, music, and other practices.

nodes.title: Mental Health in Pop Culture
nodes.description: Review of literature, film, art, television, music, podcasts, etc. to understand presentations of mental illness and their treatments. Themes such as stigma, stereotyping, vulnerability, psychiatric nosology, the doctor-patient relationship and celebrity culture will be examined. Presentations of neurodiversity will be compared and contrasted with the Western medical worldview and through the lens of entertainment.
nodes.other_requirements: Open to Degree and PACE students

nodes.title: Intro to Medical Imaging
nodes.description: Medical imaging plays a central role in healthcare delivery. Students will learn about X-Ray, CT, MRI, PET, and ultrasound imaging. Their clinical applications and role in healthcare both in the US and around the world will be discussed.
